_meta.method: lost = {word.lower() for visible live cards} - {word.lower() for visible wikt cards}, using bench_deck_quality.visible(). For each lost word, searched the wikt INDEX for that word text under any id (any lemma split), not just the id used on live -- lemma splits are a known live/wikt artifact. Bucket (a) = word absent from wikt index under every lemma split; dug into sense_menu/wiktionary.json, sense_assignments/wiktionary.json, sense_assignments_lemma/wiktionary.json and word_routing.json exclude.* to find where it fell out, and checked method priority against the Spanish min-priority=50 builder cut. Buckets (b)/(c)/(d) = word IS in the wikt index (under some id) but that id fails visible(): (b) a master boolean flag hides it, (c) all senses are blank/POS=X, (d) some other visible() condition (all-PROPN senses, cognate_score, corpus_count<=1, or an unexplained edge).

d_cognate_score.root_cause: NOT a Wiktionary sense-quality issue. The LIVE index file (BadBunnyvocabulary.index.json, built Jul 7) has ZERO entries (0/11198) carrying a cognate_score field -- it predates the assembler code path that stamps cognate_score onto the index from the shared Data/Spanish/layers/cognates.json cognate database. The WIKT index (rebuilt Jul 12 for cutover prep) DOES stamp it: 3010/11162 entries (27%) carry cognate_score, and bench_deck_quality.visible() hides any id with cognate_score >= 0.85. Data/Spanish/layers/cognates.json itself is old (Apr 20, predates the wikt cutover work) and has a meaningful false-positive rate from its CogNet+Gemini voting process. Most dramatic example: 'estar|estar' -> {score: 1.0, cognet: true}, which alone hides ALL ~20 conjugated forms of the verb 'to be' (estoy, está, estaban, estuve, ...) -- one of the most basic verbs in the language. Rebuilding the LIVE deck today with the current assembler, independent of any wiktionary/spanishdict decision, would trigger this same loss.
d_cognate_score.verdict: MOSTLY REAL LOSSES, and the most urgent finding in this report. Sampling the 237 words: roughly half look like defensible Latinate cognates the live deck's own is_transparent_cognate filter would probably also have caught if it had ever been recomputed for these ids (acción/action, universidad/university, generación/generation, presidente/president, invitación/invitation). The other half are false positives that hide basic, essential vocabulary: the entire 'estar' (to be) conjugation paradigm, besos (kisses), bonito (pretty), botas (boots), caballero (gentleman), hombres (men), mama, mucho/mucha/muchos/muchas, mover and its forms (mueve, muevete -- very common in reggaeton dance lyrics), gemela (twin), estrellas (stars), puto, polvo, suave, santa, velas, escoria, banda, idiota, franco. This is a pipeline/data-quality bug in the shared cognate database and/or the 0.85 threshold, not an artifact of the wikt cutover specifically -- but it will surface as soon as ANY deck is rebuilt with the current tooling, so it should be fixed before or alongside the wikt cutover, not treated as a wikt-only issue.

c_zero_real_senses.root_cause: These ids exist in the wikt index/master but never got a Wiktionary sense-menu entry or a classifier assignment at all (checked sense_menu/wiktionary.json and sense_assignments/wiktionary.json directly -- both False for e.g. abrazos, besan, condones). In several cases the master's 'lemma' field equals the inflected surface form itself (e.g. abrazos -> lemma 'abrazos', not 'abrazo'; besan -> lemma 'besan', not 'besar') -- the wiktionary-track lemma/derivation mapping (step_7a) failed to reduce these inflected forms to a base lemma that has a real Wiktionary headword, so no sense candidates were ever available to assign.
c_zero_real_senses.verdict: Real losses -- these are ordinary words (besan, condones, abrazos) that should have a gloss.

d_corpus_count.root_cause: The wikt-track lemmatizer splits these words into MORE distinct lemma ids than the live (SpanishDict) track did. E.g. live consolidates all 'acerca' occurrences under one id (lemma=acercar, corpus_count=2); wikt splits it into two ids (lemma=acerca, corpus_count=1 AND lemma=acercar, corpus_count=1) -- each individually fails the corpus_count>1 visibility gate even though the combined frequency clears it. Confirmed on all 8 words (acerca, acoso, despierto, guardado, maestra, mandado, marco, querido): every wikt candidate id for these words has corpus_count<=1, split across 2 ids where live had 1.
d_corpus_count.verdict: Real losses caused by corpus-count fragmentation from over-granular lemma splitting, not a sense-quality problem.

a_excluded_by_routing.root_cause: word_routing.json's exclude.cognate bucket (shared across both decks, not sense-source-specific) currently lists adversario, anime, candela, latino. Checked: their LIVE master flags are all False (is_transparent_cognate=False, etc) -- the live deck predates this routing update and still shows them. The wikt deck is running against the CURRENT routing file.
a_excluded_by_routing.verdict: ACCEPTABLE -- these are genuine cognates/loanwords (adversario/adversary, anime is literally the English word, candela/candle, latino/latino). The live deck is the stale one here; wikt's hiding them is arguably correct/current behavior, not a loss.

d_all_propn_senses.root_cause: The wikt id for these words got a single PROPN (proper-noun) sense that crowds out the common-word reading, hiding the whole card since visible() drops all-PROPN cards. 'como' is the standout: its wikt master id (227610) has exactly one sense, {pos: PROPN, translation: 'Como'} (a place name / given name reading) -- yet sense_assignments/wiktionary.json shows Gemini (flash-lite-wiktionary) DID correctly classify 'como' with a real sense (id 400, 7 examples). That classified sense never made it into the final master entry for this id -- looks like a step_8b homograph-merge bug where the PROPN candidate senses win precedence over a correctly classified common-word sense.
d_all_propn_senses.verdict: Mixed. 'como' is a real, serious loss -- it is one of the most frequent function words in Spanish (like/as/how) and its correct classification exists but got discarded by the builder. barrios, cantos, cela, champaña, evita, piscis are lower-frequency and plausibly do have a dominant proper-noun reading in this corpus, so those are more defensible, but worth a second look.

b_master_flag.root_cause: melón: is_transparent_cognate=True on the wikt master, False on the live master -- a flag divergence between the two master builds for the same id. melón is spelled almost identically to 'melon' (true cognate), but it's also in bench_deck_quality.py's DETECTOR_KNOWN_OK curated-keep list from a prior manual review ('real vocab worth teaching despite tripping detectors').
b_master_flag.verdict: Borderline / Josh's call -- legitimate cognate spelling-wise, but previously curated as worth keeping.
